message:
fix(tests): actually consume rate-limit tokens via call() after poll_ready()

The rate-limiter tests were calling poll_ready() to reserve a token but never
following up with call() to actually consume it. In the tower-style Service
contract, poll_ready() reserves capacity and call() consumes it. Without the
call(), the token was never deducted from the bucket, so assertions about
remaining tokens and pending state were testing an incomplete code path.

repro_service_rate_limit.rs:
- Add `let _ = svc.call(())` after the first poll_ready() so the second
  poll_ready() correctly sees an empty bucket and returns Pending.

service_verification.rs:
- Add `let _ = rl.call(())` after every poll_ready() in verify_009 (token
  consumption), verify_010 (refill), and verify_011 (bucket cap) so
  available_tokens() reflects actual consumption rather than just reservation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
